title: Should I Hire a Web Designer or a Web Developer?
excerpt: Many business owners confuse web designers and web developers — but they do very different things. This post explains exactly what each role covers, where they overlap, and how to decide which professional (or combination) your project needs.
content:
One of the most common sources of confusion for business owners venturing into the digital world is this: Do I need a web designer or a web developer? The titles sound similar, but the roles are distinctly different — and hiring the wrong one can lead to frustration, wasted money, and a project that doesn't deliver what you expected.

Let's clear this up once and for all.

What Does a Web Designer Do?

A web designer is primarily concerned with how a website looks and feels. Their work lives at the intersection of visual design and user experience (UX). Key responsibilities include:

  • Creating the visual layout of a website
  • Choosing color palettes, typography, and imagery
  • Designing wireframes and mockups
  • Ensuring the design is intuitive and user-friendly
  • Maintaining brand consistency across all pages
Web designers typically work in tools like Figma, Adobe XD, or Sketch. They may hand off their designs to a developer, or in some cases build simple sites using platforms like Webflow or WordPress.

What Does a Web Developer Do?

A web developer is responsible for building and coding a website. They take a design (or a brief) and turn it into a functioning site. There are two main types:

  • Front-End Developer: Codes what users see — HTML, CSS, and JavaScript that bring designs to life in the browser.
  • Back-End Developer: Builds the server-side logic, databases, and APIs that power a site's functionality.
  • Full-Stack Developer: Does both front-end and back-end work.

Which One Do You Need?

Here's a quick guide:

Your NeedHire This
You need a beautiful, strategic designWeb Designer
You need a site coded from existing designsWeb Developer
You need both design and buildBoth, or a Full-Stack team
You need a simple site on WordPress/WebflowWeb Designer with CMS experience
You need a complex web appFull-Stack Developer + UX Designer

The Best Solution: A Full-Service Team

For most businesses, the cleanest path to a great website is working with a team that handles both design and development under one roof. That way, you have seamless communication, a unified creative vision, and accountability at every stage.

That's exactly what Bluewaves Technology offers. Our multidisciplinary team includes designers, developers, and strategists who work together to build websites that look great AND perform brilliantly.

Bottom Line

Don't let confusion over job titles delay your project. Whether you need a designer, a developer, or a full team — understanding the difference helps you ask better questions, set better expectations, and find the right partner for the job.
